auth_access_model: The visible SPS web application is public, but the surfaced material is legacy and browser-oriented rather than a documented JSON API.
data_domains: spawning abundance and related demographic parameters

discovery_search.note: The site offers data and map query flows, but not a documented public search API.

metadata_schema.note: The site describes population-summary fields, but not a formal machine-readable schema.

entity_lookup.note: Populations can be selected by recovery domain, ESU, and population group in the UI.

identifier_crosswalks.note: The site is not a crosswalk service.

abundance_assessment.note: The site exists to provide salmon population summary and demographic data used in recovery planning.

telemetry_passage.note: Telemetry and passage workflows are not part of SPS.

hatchery.note: Hatchery data are not the core surface here, even though some fields reference hatchery-origin fish.

harvest_fishery.note: The public description includes harvest reduction fields, but not a broader fishery platform.

genetics_gsi.note: Genetics and GSI workflows are not exposed.

package_metadata_export.note: No package/export workflow is documented on the public page.

provenance_versioning.note: The site keeps archived database selections, but not a rich version contract.

bulk_access_api_ergonomics.note: The information is accessible, but the surface is legacy web UI rather than a clean API.

blockers: The public surface is a legacy web app, not a modern documented API.
The scaffold only fetches page/help content and does not reverse-engineer private query endpoints.
governance_constraints: Treat SPS as browser-first recovery-planning context rather than a general-purpose machine API.
